questionHtml: Suggest why water vapour deviates significantly from ideal behaviour when the gases are cooled, while nitrogen does not.
markschemeHtml:
Any two of:
nitrogen non-polar/London/dispersion forces AND water polar/H-bonding ✔
water has «much» stronger intermolecular forces ✔
water molecules attract/condense/occupy smaller volume «and therefore deviate from ideal behaviour» ✔

meta.title: Suggest why water vapour deviates significantly from ideal behaviour when the gases are cooled, while nitrogen does not.
